description: T605 TenantProvisioningOrchestrator MUST enforce: SETNX at ORDER 1 (F1521), synchronous bulkSeed (no async scatter), storeDocument before enqueue (DNA-8), TenantProvisioningFailed on all error paths, tenantId from ALS only.
blockConditions: T605 does not call SETNX or provision lock at ORDER 1
T605 uses Promise.all or Promise.allSettled for bulkSeed operations
T605 calls enqueue(TenantProvisioned) before storeDocument(tenantRecord)
T605 accepts tenantId from request payload instead of AsyncLocalStorage
T605 returns DataProcessResult.failure without emitting TenantProvisioningFailed
passConditions: T605 acquires provision lock via F1521 at executionOrder=1 before any write
T605 calls bulkSeed synchronously — all FREEDOM defaults seeded before proceeding
T605 storeDocument(tenantRecord) via F1519 before enqueue(TenantProvisioned)
T605 resolves tenantId from this.tenantContext.getTenantId() — no tenantId parameter
TenantProvisioningFailed emitted on all error paths before DataProcessResult.failure return

description: T605 TenantProvisioningOrchestrator MUST acquire provision lock before any write. Concurrent provisioning for the same tenantId MUST be blocked by the lock — not allowed to produce duplicate tenant records in xiigen-tenants.
blockConditions: T605 creates duplicate tenant records for the same tenantId under concurrent load
T605 does not check for existing provision lock before proceeding with tenant creation
xiigen-tenants index contains two records with identical tenantId after concurrent provisioning
passConditions: T605 acquires provision lock (SETNX) at ORDER 1 before checking for existing tenant
Concurrent provisioning request for same tenantId is rejected with TenantProvisioningFailed — lock already held
Only one tenant record per tenantId in xiigen-tenants after any provisioning scenario

description: T605 TenantProvisioningOrchestrator MUST seed FREEDOM defaults from FREEDOM config keys (tenant_default_quota_api_calls, tenant_default_quota_storage_gb, tenant_default_quota_seats). Hardcoded default values are prohibited — defaults must be operator-tunable.
blockConditions: T605 uses hardcoded default quota values instead of FREEDOM config keys
T605 does not seed FREEDOM defaults during provisioning
T605 seeds FREEDOM defaults asynchronously with no completion guarantee
passConditions: T605 reads freedom defaults from tenant_default_quota_api_calls, tenant_default_quota_storage_gb, tenant_default_quota_seats config keys
FREEDOM defaults seeded synchronously before TenantProvisioned emitted
No hardcoded default values in T605 provisioning path

description: T605 TenantProvisioningOrchestrator MUST resolve tenantId exclusively from AsyncLocalStorage. Any T605 implementation accepting tenantId as a request payload parameter enables tenant impersonation.
blockConditions: T605 method signature includes tenantId as a parameter
T605 reads tenantId from event payload, request body, or query parameter
T605 validates tenantId from ALS against payload tenantId — payload tenantId should not exist at all
passConditions: T605 calls this.tenantContext.getTenantId() or equivalent ALS accessor for tenantId
No tenantId parameter in T605 method signatures or event handler parameters
Fabric middleware sets ALS context before T605 handler is invoked

description: T605 TenantProvisioningOrchestrator MUST emit both TenantProvisioned (success) and TenantProvisioningFailed (failure) events. Missing either event leaves downstream consumers without lifecycle signals.
blockConditions: T605 success path does not emit TenantProvisioned event
T605 failure path does not emit TenantProvisioningFailed event before returning
T605 emits TenantProvisioned before storeDocument(tenantRecord) completes — DNA-8 violation
passConditions: T605 emits TenantProvisioned with tenantId, tier, provisionedAt, and freedomSeedCount after successful storeDocument
T605 emits TenantProvisioningFailed with failureCode and failureReason on all error paths
TenantProvisioned only emitted after storeDocument(tenantRecord) returns successfully

description: T605 TenantProvisioningOrchestrator MUST use ITenantRegistryService (F1519), ITenantSeedService (F1520), IProvisionLockService (F1521), and IFreedomConfigService (F1522). Direct database access without fabric interfaces violates Rule 1.
blockConditions: T605 imports database SDK directly instead of using fabric interfaces
T605 does not inject IProvisionLockService (F1521) for lock operations
T605 does not inject ITenantSeedService (F1520) for FREEDOM default seeding
passConditions: T605 injects F1519 ITenantRegistryService for tenant record storage
T605 injects F1520 ITenantSeedService for synchronous FREEDOM default seeding
T605 injects F1521 IProvisionLockService for SETNX provision lock
T605 injects F1522 IFreedomConfigService for default config value retrieval

description: T605 generation prompt MUST include discriminating constraints: SETNX_IDEMPOTENCY machine component, ATOMIC_PROVISION_SEQUENCE enforces ORDER 1 lock then synchronous seed then store then enqueue, PROVISIONING_STATUS_MARKER for in-flight status tracking.
blockConditions: Generation prompt omits SETNX_IDEMPOTENCY machine component directive
Generation prompt does not specify synchronous bulkSeed requirement
Generation prompt does not specify storeDocument before enqueue (DNA-8) for TenantProvisioned
passConditions: Generation prompt includes SETNX_IDEMPOTENCY machine component with ORDER 1 directive
Generation prompt specifies bulkSeed as synchronous — no Promise.all scatter
Generation prompt specifies DNA-8: storeDocument before enqueue pattern for TenantProvisioned

description: T606 TenantConfigurationManager MUST enforce: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS as compile-time constant, ORDER 1 locked key check with no storage on rejection, OCC enabled on all config writes, tenantId from ALS only, ConfigKeyImmutable emitted on rejection.
blockConditions: T606 reads MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS from FREEDOM config or database at runtime
T606 proceeds to storeDocument before checking MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS
T606 calls storeDocument without OCC option — { occ: true } absent
T606 accepts tenantId from request payload
T606 silently ignores locked key write attempt without emitting ConfigKeyImmutable
passConditions: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS defined as compile-time const Set at module level in T606
T606 calls validateKeyNotLocked(key) at executionOrder=1 — returns failure + ConfigKeyImmutable before any storage
All T606 storeDocument calls include { occ: true } option
T606 resolves tenantId from ALS — no tenantId parameter in method signature
ConfigKeyImmutable event emitted with rejectedKey and MACHINE_LOCKED_KEY reason on every locked key rejection

description: T606 TenantConfigurationManager MUST enforce the custom key quota from FREEDOM config (tenant_config_max_custom_keys). A tenant exceeding the maximum custom key count must be rejected before any write attempt.
blockConditions: T606 does not enforce tenant_config_max_custom_keys from FREEDOM config
T606 allows unlimited custom config keys without quota enforcement
T606 counts existing keys after the write rather than before — write exceeds quota before detection
passConditions: T606 reads tenant_config_max_custom_keys from FREEDOM config

description: T606 TenantConfigurationManager MUST emit TenantConfigurationUpdated with occVersion after every successful config write. Downstream audit consumers rely on occVersion to detect concurrent modification conflicts.
blockConditions: T606 does not emit TenantConfigurationUpdated after successful config write
TenantConfigurationUpdated event missing occVersion field
T606 emits TenantConfigurationUpdated before storeDocument completes — DNA-8 violation
passConditions: T606 emits TenantConfigurationUpdated with configKey, updatedAt, updatedBy, and occVersion after storeDocument success
occVersion in event matches the version returned by the successful OCC write
TenantConfigurationUpdated only emitted after storeDocument returns successfully

description: T606 TenantConfigurationManager MUST protect engine-critical keys (MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS) from tenant writes. Runtime-configurable locked key lists are a security vulnerability — operators can accidentally or maliciously remove keys from protection.
blockConditions: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS list can be modified at runtime via any mechanism
T606 allows a superadmin bypass path that skips MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS enforcement
Locked key check can be disabled via FREEDOM config flag
passConditions: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS defined as compile-time const — immutable at runtime
No bypass path in T606 that skips locked key enforcement — enforcement applies to all callers
MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS enforcement cannot be toggled via FREEDOM config or environment variable

description: T606 TenantConfigurationManager MUST emit both TenantConfigurationUpdated (success) and ConfigKeyImmutable (locked key rejection). Both events required for downstream audit completeness.
blockConditions: T606 success path does not emit TenantConfigurationUpdated
T606 locked key rejection does not emit ConfigKeyImmutable
T606 emits TenantConfigurationUpdated even when OCC conflict occurs — no event on failure
passConditions: TenantConfigurationUpdated emitted with occVersion after every successful config write

description: T606 TenantConfigurationManager MUST inject ITenantConfigStoreService (F1523) and IFreedomConfigService (F1522). OCC write capability comes from F1523 — direct database SDK use bypasses OCC enforcement.
blockConditions: T606 does not inject ITenantConfigStoreService (F1523) for config storage

T606 reads tenant_config_max_custom_keys without using IFreedomConfigService (F1522)

description: T606 generation prompt MUST include: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS_COMPILE_TIME machine component (const Set at module level), OCC_WRITE_ONLY (all writes via F1523 with occ:true), ALS_TENANT_ISOLATION (no tenantId parameter).
blockConditions: Generation prompt specifies MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS as runtime-configurable
Generation prompt omits OCC_WRITE_ONLY machine component directive

description: T607 TenantQuotaMaterializer MUST enforce: Redis MULTI/EXEC for all quota keys in one atomic transaction, values from FREEDOM tier definitions only, quota key filter to exclude non-quota keys, keyCount in TenantQuotaMaterialized event.
blockConditions: T607 uses individual SET operations in a loop instead of MULTI/EXEC
T607 uses hardcoded quota values instead of tier definitions from FREEDOM config
T607 does not filter keys before MULTI/EXEC — writes to non-quota Redis keys
TenantQuotaMaterialized event missing keyCount field
passConditions: T607 builds complete quotaKeyMap from tier definitions before issuing MULTI/EXEC
T607 calls redis.MULTI() then chains all SET operations then EXEC() atomically
T607 applies QUOTA_KEY_FILTER before MULTI/EXEC to exclude non-quota keys
TenantQuotaMaterialized event includes keyCount matching number of keys written in EXEC

description: T607 TenantQuotaMaterializer MUST read quota values from FREEDOM config key tenant_quota_tier_{tier}. Quota values are operator-tunable business decisions — hardcoding prevents tier adjustments without code deployment.
blockConditions: T607 uses hardcoded constants for quota values (api_calls: 10000, storage_gb: 50, etc.)
T607 does not read tier definition from FREEDOM config
T607 applies the same quota values regardless of tenant tier

description: T607 TenantQuotaMaterializer MUST apply QUOTA_KEY_FILTER to restrict MULTI/EXEC writes to quota-prefixed keys only. Writing to arbitrary Redis keys during quota materialization could overwrite session or lock data.

description: T607 TenantQuotaMaterializer MUST emit TenantQuotaMaterialized with keyCount after successful EXEC. Downstream quota monitors use keyCount to detect incomplete materializations and trigger re-run.
blockConditions: T607 does not emit TenantQuotaMaterialized after successful EXEC

keyCount in event equals the number of quota keys written in the MULTI/EXEC transaction

description: T608 TenantLifecycleManager MUST enforce: suspend uses updateDocument only (no deleteDocument), TenantSuspended carries cascadeToSubscriptions:true, DataPurgeRequested only on termination (never suspension), tombstoneRef is SHA-256 hash of tenantId (never raw tenantId), tenantId from ALS only.

tombstoneRef computed as crypto.createHash('sha256').update(tenantId).digest('hex') — raw tenantId absent from events
